disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The white patch is completely asymptomatic — no pain, no itch, no burning
trigger_question_examples: Does the patch itself hurt, itch or burn at all?
When you touch the white area, is it painful or numb?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, that's the strange thing — the white patch doesn't hurt or itch or burn at all. It feels like nothing. It just looks terrifying.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The surrounding redness appeared only after the patient rubbed and pressed the area repeatedly tonight
trigger_question_examples: When did the redness around it appear?
Have you been touching or rubbing the area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The redness wasn't there when I first saw it — it came up after I kept rubbing and pressing on it tonight because I was panicking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no systemic illness; the patient feels otherwise completely well
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel unwell in yourself, or have you had a fever?
Any chills, feeling hot and cold, or feeling generally sick?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I feel completely fine in myself — no fever, no feeling sick. It's just this patch that has frightened me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesion was discovered accidentally tonight; the patient cannot say how long it has actually been present
trigger_question_examples: How long have you had it?
Is this the first time you've noticed it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I only saw it tonight by accident when I was changing — but honestly I have no idea how long it might have actually been there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No open skin, no bleeding, no ulcer, no black or dark tissue on the patch
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin broken open anywhere, or bleeding?
Is any part of it black, dark or crusted?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's not broken or bleeding, and it's not black — it's just white and shiny, like the surface is thinned.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is intensely frightened and fixated, interpreting the appearance as dying flesh that is spreading
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this might be?
How are you feeling about it right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm terrified. It looks like my flesh is dying and I'm convinced it's spreading — I keep checking it and I can't calm down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new similar pale patches elsewhere, including the genital area, that the patient is aware of
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any similar pale patches anywhere else on your body?
Any white patches in the genital area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Not that I've seen — I've only noticed this one on my shoulder. I haven't looked everywhere though.
